title: Herbal Cough Soothers: 10 Top Picks
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cough are a natural remedy that helps alleviate cough symptoms by soothing the throat and reducing inflammation. They are made from various herbs, including Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Sambucus nigra, Mentha x piperita, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Verbascum thapsus. If left untreated, a persistent cough can disrupt sleep, lead to fatigue, and even worsen respiratory conditions, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a good remedy for cough because it has special properties that help relieve congestion. The main ingredient, eucalyptol, is a natural decongestant that opens up airways and makes it easier to breathe. This is especially helpful when you have a cold or flu and your airways are blocked. When you drink eucalyptus tea, the eucalyptol is absorbed into your body and starts to break down mucus and phlegm. This helps to clear out your airways and reduce the severity of your cough. Additionally, eucalyptus tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce discomfort. Drinking eucalyptus tea regularly can also help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the need to cough as much. This can provide quick relief and make it easier to manage a cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Eucalyptol is good for cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and irritation.
Quercetin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold.

Tannins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort caused by a cough.
Cineole is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing herbal tea from Lavandula angustifolia that helps with cough, start by gathering fresh or dried lavender flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them with clean water and pat dry with a paper towel. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of the lavender flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the herbal properties of the lavender. Strain the tea into a separate cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the used lavender flowers. You can add honey to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as lavender tea can be quite potent. Finally, drink the tea while it's still warm to help calm your cough and soothe your throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. This tea comes from a plant that has been used for centuries to fight infections and boost the immune system. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, its active ingredients, like alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to reduce inflammation in your throat and airways, which can help calm a cough. It also has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the bacteria and viruses that can cause a cough. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the underlying cause of the cough, Echinacea purpurea tea can help you feel better and recover faster. Additionally, its soothing properties can help calm your throat and make it easier to swallow, providing relief from a cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Tannins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Flavonoids help to soothe a cough by reducing inflammation and opening up airways, making it easier to breathe and relax.
Alkylamides are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, which in turn helps to relieve coughing.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, you will need dried lavender flowers. Start by boiling a pot of fresh water, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to the pot, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup, and discard the flowers. You can add honey if you want a sweeter taste. Lavender tea is soothing and can help calm a sore throat and ease coughs. Another way to make the tea is to use cold infusion method, where you add the lavender flowers to a pitcher of cold water and let it steep in the refrigerator overnight. This method is better for people who can't tolerate hot tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a popular remedy for soothing coughs. The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow, providing relief from a cough. The tea also has expectorant properties, which means it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. Glycyrrhizin also has antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing the cough. When consumed as a tea, licorice root can also provide a soothing, warm sensation to the throat, helping to calm a cough and provide relief from discomfort.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for cough because it helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the irritation that causes coughing.
Saponins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, thereby soothing and relieving coughs.
Flavonoids are good for cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the throat, reducing coughing and irritation.
Polyphenols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the throat, reducing the discomfort and irritation associated with coughing.
Tannins are good for cough because they help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort and irritation caused by coughing.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, start by gathering a handful of dried lavender flowers. You can buy these at a health food store or online. Next, boil a kettle of fresh water and let it cool slightly. Then, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Place the infuser or bag into a cup and pour the cooled water over the lavender. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which is the time it takes for the lavender's healing properties to release into the water. After the steeping time, carefully remove the infuser or bag and discard the lavender flowers. Your Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea is now ready to drink. You can add honey to sweeten it if you like, but be sure to drink it while it's still warm as this will help soothe your cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy for cough. The leaves of the thyme plant contain a powerful antioxidant called thymol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink thyme tea, the thymol helps to soothe an irritated throat and calm a cough. Thyme tea also contains other compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. This can be especially helpful for people with respiratory infections, such as bronchitis or pneumonia. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, thyme tea can help to relieve a cough and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off the underlying infection that is causing the cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and ease coughing.
Carvacrol is good for cough because it has antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the throat, making it easier to breathe and calm a cough.

Rosmarinic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea to help with cough, start by gathering a handful of dried lavender flowers. You can buy these at a health food store or online. Next, boil fresh water in a pot and let it cool for a minute. Then, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a cup and pour the cooled water over them. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, so the lavender's healing properties can release into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea into another cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to remove the lavender flowers. You can add honey to sweeten the tea, if desired. To use this tea for cough relief, drink it warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day. Be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea, made from Zingiber officinale, is a popular remedy for coughs because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. The tea also contains compounds that loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to cough up. When you drink ginger tea, the warmth of the liquid helps ease a sore throat, while its antimicrobial properties fight off infections that can cause coughs. Additionally, ginger tea has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can be helpful if you have a cough that is related to stomach issues. Drinking ginger tea can also help ease congestion and promote better sleep, which can help alleviate cough symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are not actually found, but rather Gingerols are a type of compound found in Ginger. However, Gingerols in Ginger are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Shogaols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to swallow and relieving discomfort and pain caused by coughing.
Volatile oils are good for cough because they have antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the throat and calm the cough.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, start by gathering a handful of fresh lavender flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried lavender buds. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool slightly. Then, add the lavender flowers or buds to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their healing properties. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the lavender flowers or buds. You can add honey to sweeten the tea if needed. To help relieve cough, drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before bedtime, as it can also promote relaxation and a good night's sleep. You can also store the tea in the fridge for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular remedy for coughs. It works by loosening mucus in the throat, making it easier to cough up. The menthol in peppermint tea helps to open up airways, reducing congestion and making breathing easier. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol cools down the throat, which can provide relief from sore throats and coughs. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce swelling. The tea's antibacterial properties also help to fight off infections that can cause coughs. As you drink peppermint tea, the menthol is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can help to relax the muscles in the throat and reduce coughing. This can provide quick relief from coughs and make it easier to breathe.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for cough because it helps to thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and soreness in the throat.
Rosmarinic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated airways and reduce the severity of coughing.
Tannins help relieve cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and throat, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, start by gathering dried lavender flowers. You can either purchase them from a health store or grow your own lavender plant and dry the flowers yourself. Next, take a small handful of the dried lavender flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Boil some water and carefully pour it over the lavender flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the lavender's soothing properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the lavender flowers. You can add honey to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as lavender can be quite potent. Once you've made the tea, let it cool down before drinking it. To help with cough, drink 1-2 cups of the lavender tea a day, ideally before bedtime to promote a restful sleep and alleviate congestion. Remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using lavender tea as a treatment for any medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tea is a popular remedy for coughs. The leaves of this plant contain a type of fiber called mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated throats. When you drink Verbascum thapsus tea, the mucilage works to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. This can bring quick relief from coughs and sore throats caused by colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce congestion and mucus production, further easing cough symptoms. By providing a protective coating for the throat and reducing inflammation, Verbascum thapsus tea can help to calm a persistent cough and promote a good night's sleep.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.
Saponins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, reducing the severity of coughing.
Flavonoids are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cough.

remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, you'll need dried lavender flowers and a teapot. Start by boiling water in the teapot, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried lavender flowers for every 8 oz of water. Let the flowers steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow their healing properties to release. Next, strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the lavender flowers. You can add honey to taste if you prefer a sweeter flavor, but be careful not to add too much as lavender can be quite strong. Once the tea has cooled slightly, drink it to help soothe a cough and calm your throat. It's recommended to drink this tea in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups a day, and to consult with a healthcare professional if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies before consuming lavender tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a good choice for cough relief. The reason is that rosemary has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink rosemary tea, these properties help to calm down an irritated throat and reduce inflammation. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow, which is especially helpful when you have a sore throat or a cough. Additionally, rosemary tea contains compounds that help to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu and are experiencing a persistent cough. By reducing inflammation and thinning out mucus, rosemary tea can help to provide quick relief from a cough, making it a popular natural remedy for people who are looking for a gentle and non-habit forming way to soothe their throat and calm their cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and cough less.
Quercetin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, making it easier to breathe and cough less.
Carnosic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cough.
Carvacrol is good for cough because it has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Tannins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, start by gathering 1-2 tablespoons of dried lavender flowers. You can buy these flowers from a health food store or grow them in your garden. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or kettle. Once the water is boiling, remove it from the heat source and pour it over the dried lavender flowers in a cup or teapot. Let the flowers steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into another cup to remove the flowers, and add honey to taste if you prefer it sweet. You can drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe your cough and promote relaxation. Note that pregnant women and people with allergies should consult a doctor before using lavender tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tea, also known as elderflower tea, is a natural remedy for a cough. The flowers of the Sambucus nigra plant contain special compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe. These compounds also have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause coughs. When you drink Sambucus nigra tea, the flavonoids and other active ingredients are absorbed into your body and work to calm your throat, making it less sore and less itchy. The tea also helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out your airways. This makes it a popular remedy for coughs, colds, and flu.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieve cough symptoms.
Tannins are good for cough because they have antitussive properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing the urge to cough and promoting a good night's sleep.
Polyphenols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with a cough, start by gathering fresh or dried lavender flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently dry them in a low-temperature oven or let them air dry for a few days. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of dried lavender flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh lavender flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the lavender's medicinal properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the lavender flowers. Then, add honey or lemon to taste, as these ingredients can help soothe a sore throat and ease the cough. Once you've enjoyed the tea, you can store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea regularly can help alleviate coughs and provide relief from respiratory issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea is good for cough because it has natural ingredients that can help soothe and calm the throat. The tea contains compounds called flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can reduce swelling in the throat and help ease the pain and discomfort caused by a cough. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that can cause a cough. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help relax the muscles in the throat and make it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people who have a persistent cough or one that is caused by a cold or flu. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea can also help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and get rid of.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to get rid of a cough.
Linalyl acetate is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and cough up excess mucus.
Apigenin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it a natural remedy to soothe and calm coughs.
Tannins are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relax.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for cough
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with cough, start by gathering a handful of dried lavender flowers. You can find these at a local health food store or online. Next, boil a pot of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add one tablespoon of dried lavender flowers to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a slice of lemon or honey to taste. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the lavender flowers. To relieve a cough, drink the tea warm, ideally three times a day. You can also mix it with other herbal teas, such as lemon balm or thyme, to create a soothing and relaxing blend. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

supplementary_best: For a cough remedy, combine Eucalyptus globulus and Thymus vulgaris with a pinch of Glycyrrhiza glabra. Eucalyptus globulus is known for its decongestant properties, which help ease congestion and reduce coughing. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, is an antimicrobial herb that fights off infections that can cause coughing. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, soothes the throat and reduces inflammation. When mixed together, these three herbs create a powerful combination that addresses multiple aspects of a cough. Eucalyptus globulus opens airways, Thymus vulgaris combats infections, and Glycyrrhiza glabra calms the throat. This blend provides a balanced approach to cough relief, making it an effective remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cough, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. One of these is Yerba Mate (Ilex paraguariensis), which is too stimulating and can irritate your throat further. Another is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), which contains a chemical called berberine that can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making your cough worse. You should also avoid Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia), which can be too harsh for your throat and cause more irritation. Additionally, Green Tea (Camellia sinensis) might not be the best choice if you're sensitive to caffeine, as it contains a small amount that can make your cough worse. Last but not least, you should be cautious with Peppermint (Agastache rugosa) as it can cause your airways to relax, leading to more congestion and discomfort.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. When consumed as a juice, ginger can be very effective in calming the throat and loosening mucus. You can also try making a ginger syrup by boiling fresh ginger in water and then mixing it with honey. This can be a great alternative to herbal teas. Another natural remedy is slippery elm, a type of tree bark that can help protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat. When mixed with water, slippery elm creates a protective barrier that reduces coughing and irritation. You can also try making a cough syrup by mixing slippery elm with honey and other natural ingredients like lemon and eucalyptus oil. Honey itself is also a great natural remedy for coughs, as it has antibacterial properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation. Eating a spoonful of pure honey before bed can help calm a cough and promote a restful night's sleep.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have a cough, they are often experiencing other health problems too. One common issue is a sore throat. This is because the cough can irritate the throat, making it sore and painful. Medicinal herbs like slippery elm and licorice root can help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. They work by coating the mucous membranes with a protective layer, making it easier to swallow and reducing discomfort.  Another issue people with a cough often face is a runny nose and congestion. This is because the body is trying to expel the irritant causing the cough, often a viral or bacterial infection, from the lungs and airways. Herbs like eucalyptus and peppermint can help ease congestion by loosening mucus and making it easier to breathe. They do this by releasing oils that break down the mucus, allowing it to be expelled more easily.  People with a cough may also experience a fever, as their body tries to fight off the underlying infection. Herbs like yarrow and elderberry can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms. They work by releasing compounds that cool the body and reduce inflammation, making it easier to recover from the infection.  Additionally, a cough can also lead to exhaustion and fatigue. This is because the body is using a lot of energy to fight off the infection and recover from the cough. Herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help boost energy levels and reduce fatigue. They do this by stimulating the body's natural energy production and reducing stress and anxiety.  Lastly, a cough can also lead to chest congestion and bronchitis. This is because the cough can irritate the lungs and airways, leading to inflammation and mucus buildup. Herbs like thyme and echinacea can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. They work by releasing compounds that fight off the underlying infection and reduce inflammation, making it easier to recover from bronchitis.
